document.title: Building Accessible React Components
document.description: Practical patterns for creating inclusive React components that meet WCAG 2.1 AA standards.

document.title: Building Scalable APIs with Go and PostgreSQL
document.description: A deep dive into designing high-throughput REST APIs using Go, PostgreSQL connection pooling, and caching strategies.

body.raw:
## Why Go for APIs?

Go's concurrency model, fast compilation, and small binary size make it an excellent choice for building API gateways and microservices. In this post, I'll share patterns I've used in production.

## Connection Pooling

One of the most common bottlenecks is database connection management. Use `pgxpool` for PostgreSQL:

```go
pool, err := pgxpool.New(ctx, os.Getenv("DATABASE_URL"))
```

## Caching Layer

Implement a two-tier cache with in-memory (LRU) and Redis for hot data.

## Rate Limiting

Use a token bucket algorithm for per-IP and per-route rate limiting.

document.title: Docker for Full Stack Developers
document.description: A practical guide to containerizing full-stack applications for local development and production deployment.

body.raw:
## Why Containerize?

Consistent environments from development to production, eliminating the "it works on my machine" problem.

## Multi-stage Builds

Use multi-stage Dockerfiles to keep production images small:

```dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci
COPY . .
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=builder /app/.next ./.next
COPY --from=builder /app/node_modules ./node_modules
EXPOSE 3000
CMD ["npm", "start"]
```

## Docker Compose for Development

Set up your entire stack — Next.js, PostgreSQL, Redis — with a single `docker compose up`.

## Tips

- Use `.dockerignore` to avoid sending `node_modules` to the Docker daemon
- Pin base image versions for reproducible builds
- Use health checks for service dependencies
